mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW X5 xDrive30d engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned X5 xDrive30d engine typically costs £2,800–£5,500 supply only depending on which generation you have (E70/F15's N57 is cheaper; G05's B57 is more expensive). A reconditioned 306D3 engine costs on average £1,417 in good condition. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,500. A full engine replacement with labour can cost £8,000–£11,000. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your vehicle.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW X5 xDrive30d engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on which generation your X5 has. The F15 30d with the N57 engine is considered the 'sweet spot for value and reliability'. The G05 xDrive30d with the B57 engine (265 PS) is the most reliable variant. The N57 has known timing chain issues, though less severe than the four-cylinder N47. Well‑serviced N57 engines with 8,000–10,000 mile oil changes can exceed 180,000 miles.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW X5 xDrive30d?

acceptedAnswer.text: On the E70/F15 (N57), the most common issue is timing chain rattle – the chain, guides, and tensioners can wear prematurely. On both N57 and B57, common problems include EGR cooler failure, DPF blockage (from urban driving), oil leaks (valve cover, oil filter housing), and AdBlue/NOx sensor issues on the B57.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing an X5 xDrive30d engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and condition. Against a tidy £8,000–£25,000 X5, a £2,800–£5,500 recon is 15–40% of the car's value – often worth it on well‑maintained examples. If the timing chain has snapped on an N57, a full engine replacement may be necessary. Get a full diagnostic assessment before committing.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my X5's engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The E70 LCI (2010–2013) uses the N57D30A. The F15 (2013–2018) uses the N57D30O0 (245 PS) or N57D30O1 (258 PS) – check your power output. The G05 (2018–present) uses the B57D30O0. The engine code is stamped on the engine block and listed on your V5C.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair an X5 xDrive30d engine issue, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most issues – N57 timing chain wear (if caught early), EGR cooler failure, DPF blockage, oil leaks – are repairable. However, if the timing chain has snapped on an N57, the engine is an interference design – pistons will strike valves. In this case, a full engine replacement is usually more cost‑effective than a rebuild. Service the oil more regularly at 8,000–10,000 mile intervals.

replacementCosts.h2: BMW X5 xDrive30d Engine Replacement Costs

rows.bestFor: Insurance jobs; warranty claims; full provenance

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the X5 xDrive30d pricing across generations. A reconditioned 306D3 engine costs on average £1,417 in good condition, with new or reconditioned engines costing up to £1,500. The N57 is generally cheaper to replace than the B57; the G05's B57 commands a premium due to its mild-hybrid technology and newer design. A full engine replacement with labour can cost £8,000–£11,000.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an X5 xDrive30d engine typically takes 12‑16 hours at £75‑£110/hr (independent BMW specialist). The X5's large SUV layout adds some complexity compared to saloon models.

cta.label: → Compare X5 xDrive30d engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: N57 Timing Chain Rattle (E70 LCI & F15)
problems.symptoms: Distinct rattling sound from the rear of the engine, particularly noticeable when the engine is revved, cold-start rattle, metallic ticking, engine warning light — catastrophic failure can occur without warning.
problems.replacementCost: £2,800–£4,800 [PLACEHOLDER] (if chain snaps and damages the engine).

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattling, book an inspection immediately.

problems.recommendation: The N57 engine is prone to excessive timing chain wear which can lead to premature failure. Symptoms can include a rattle on start-up or a change in engine note where the chain is hitting on the guides — sometimes they give you no warning at all. The timing chain is located at the rear of the engine on these BMW diesels, making access labour-intensive. Service the oil more regularly at 8,000–10,000 mile intervals. A £1,500–£3,000 preventative chain replacement is far cheaper than a £2,800–£4,800 engine replacement. At the first sign of rattling, have the timing system inspected immediately.

problems.title: EGR Cooler & DPF Blockage (N57 & B57)
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss without visible external leaks, white smoke from exhaust, reduced power, poor fuel economy, warning messages, rough idle at cold start, black smoke, sluggish acceleration, limp mode.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (if caught early; ignored coolant loss can cause engine damage).

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if coolant loss, white smoke, or warning messages occur.

problems.recommendation: EGR and DPF blockages on the xDrive30d are common, predictable results of short trips and age. Diesel X5 models, particularly the 30d, can develop soot build-up in the DPF or EGR system if driven mostly on short trips. There have been recalls for faulty EGR replacement. Regular motorway runs (30+ minutes at 60+ mph) are essential to maintain the exhaust filtration system. If you notice coolant loss with no visible external leak, have the EGR system inspected promptly.

problems.title: Oil Leaks — Valve Cover & Oil Filter Housing (N57 & B57)
problems.symptoms: Oil spots under the vehicle, burning oil smell, low oil level warning, visible oil leak from the top or front of the engine.
problems.repairCost: £300–£900 [PLACEHOLDER] (valve cover gasket or oil filter housing gasket replacement).
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (if caught early; ignored oil loss can lead to engine damage).

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if oil leak is detected.

problems.recommendation: Many X5s experience oil leaks, particularly from the valve cover gasket and oil filter housing. The B57 engine also suffers from oil leaks, often originating from the valve cover gasket or oil filter housing. If the oil filter housing gasket fails, oil can leak onto the serpentine belt, causing it to slip or break — which can then be ingested into the engine through the front crankshaft seal, causing catastrophic failure. A £300–£900 gasket replacement is far cheaper than the consequences of a snapped belt.

problems.title: B57 AdBlue / NOx Sensor Issues (G05, 2018–present)
problems.symptoms: Engine warning light, reduced power, AdBlue warnings, failed emissions test, poor fuel economy.

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon if warning lights appear.

problems.recommendation: The B57 engine uses an AdBlue (SCR) system to reduce NOx emissions. Common issues include NOx sensor failure and AdBlue system faults. Regular servicing and using high-quality AdBlue can help prevent issues. If the engine warning light appears with emissions-related codes, have the system diagnosed promptly.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: BMW X5 xDrive30d — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Catch it early — cheaper than engine replacement
Full engine replacement — valve damage is almost certain
Worth fixing — common and well‑understood repair
Full engine replacement often more cost‑effective

buyingChecks.title: Buying This BMW X5 xDrive30d? Check These First
buyIf: * Full BMW service history with documented oil changes (8,000–10,000 mile intervals are essential)
* E70/F15 (N57): Timing chain inspected and any work documented — this is critical on higher‑mileage cars
* G05 (B57): Evidence of regular motorway driving (DPF‑friendly usage) and AdBlue system maintenance
* A well‑maintained example with lower mileage — the F15 30d with the N57 engine is the sweet spot for value and reliability
avoidIf: * E70/F15 (N57): Any sign of cold‑start rattle — timing chain warning
* G05 (B57): Any sign of AdBlue warnings or emissions system faults
* No evidence of regular oil changes — both engines are intolerant of neglect
* DPF warnings on the dashboard — indicates urban‑only driving

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The BMW X5 xDrive30d represents the best-selling diesel variant in BMW's large SUV range — and for good reason. The F15 generation with the N57 engine is considered the "sweet spot for value and reliability". The G05's B57 is the most reliable variant, with BMW having "consistently improved upon diesel technologies". The N57 has known timing chain issues, though less severe than the four-cylinder N47. Buy on service history above all else — a vehicle with documented timing chain maintenance and regular oil changes is a safe bet; a neglected example can be expensive to repair. As one specialist advised: "service the oil more regularly (8-10k intervals)".

cta.label: → Compare X5 xDrive30d engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW X5 xDrive30d (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: 1. Cold Start — Any rattle? (N57 timing chain warning on E70/F15 — stop immediately)
2. Service History — Regular oil changes (8,000–10,000 miles) — this is non‑negotiable
3. Timing Chain History — E70/F15 (N57): Has the chain been inspected or replaced?
4. EGR / DPF Health — Has the vehicle done regular longer runs? (short trips kill DPFs)
5. AdBlue System — G05 (B57): Any AdBlue warnings? Any emissions system faults?
6. Oil Leaks — Check under the vehicle and around the valve cover and oil filter housing
7. Coolant Level — Any sign of coolant loss? (EGR cooler warning)
8. MOT History — Check for emissions‑related failures or any advisory notes
